behavior_in_task_description.reason: All file paths, schema keys (filter, requestHeaders, requestBody, static), CLI flags (--filter, --request-headers, --request-body), label strings ('Request headers:', 'Request body:'), and the copilot-instructions.md content are all explicitly stated in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what params to add and output format but doesn't show the code diff; agent must figure out how to implement filter logic, plumb params through renderRequest, etc.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for schema entries via regex, presence of CLI flag strings, and use self-contained JS that replicates the logic rather than importing gold helpers; multiple implementations would pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: test_render_request_headers_and_body and test_filter_regex_applied execute JS via tsx; test_syntax_check runs node --check.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests check schema params exist, copilot-instructions content exists, and behavioral JS replicates logic — a stub returning defaults wouldn't pass schema regex checks or filename creation.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Filter, requestHeaders, requestBody, static rename, CLI flags, and copilot-instructions creation are all tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are named descriptively (test_schema_has_new_params, test_filter_regex_applied, etc.) with docstrings explaining purpose.

structured_data_schema.reason: Output format for headers/body labels and indentation is explicitly documented in instruction.md.

no_typos.reason: File paths and names match between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction points to files (network.ts, commands.ts) which are appropriately scoped; doesn't name exact line numbers.
